response.justification: The candidate is a genuinely senior generalist engineer with nearly two decades of full-stack, backend, and systems experience, plus strong teaching and workshop-facilitation skills that fit the customer-facing side of a forward-deployed role. However, the role requires 8+ years building production-grade AI solutions, and his AI exposure is limited to roughly six months of MCP/agentic-tooling work at re:cinq. There is no demonstrated experience with vector databases, RAG architectures, evaluation pipelines, LLM optimization, or multi-agent frameworks, and Python is absent from his skills list, leaving the core requirements largely unmet.
strengths: 19+ years of broad production engineering experience across web, backend, embedded, and high-throughput systems (millions of requests/sec at Dunnhumby)
Recent hands-on exposure to MCP code generators and agentic-workflow infrastructure at re:cinq
Strong communication and mentoring track record via workshops, courses, and conference talks — relevant to customer-facing discovery and enablement
concerns: Falls far short of the required 8+ years building AI-driven solutions; GenAI experience is ~6 months
No evidence of vector databases, RAG architectures, evaluation pipelines, or multi-agent frameworks (LangGraph/CrewAI), and Python is not listed among skills
Cloud experience appears limited to product-level AWS CDK usage rather than designing AI systems on major cloud platforms

key_factors.factor: Insufficient AI/GenAI experience vs. hard requirement
Deep general engineering seniority and customer-facing skills
